questions.learningObjective: Classify VOR, ILS, LOC, GS, and GPS as navigation systems.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of radio systems including VOR, ILS, localizer, glideslope, and GPS, the technician shall classify them collectively as navigation guidance systems.
questions.targetSourceStem: VOR, ILS, localizer, glideslope, and GPS are components of what aircraft system category?

flashcard.definition: They are all navigation guidance systems used to direct the aircraft along a desired course.

jeopardy.clue: VOR, ILS, localizer, glideslope, and GPS all fall under this single aircraft system category.

multipleChoice.stem: VOR, ILS, localizer, glideslope, and GPS are all components of which category of aircraft system?

practice.stem: An apprentice asks how to categorize VOR, ILS, localizer, glideslope, and GPS on a systems diagram. Which single category should he place them under?

preassessment.stem: VOR, ILS, localizer, glideslope, and GPS belong to which aircraft system category?

questions.learningObjective: Identify the frequency range of VHF aircraft communication radios.
questions.fullObjective: Given a question about VHF aircraft COM radios, the technician shall identify the operating band as 118.000 MHz to 136.975 MHz.
questions.targetSourceStem: VHF aircraft communication radios operate in what frequency range?

flashcard.definition: 118.000 MHz through 136.975 MHz, in 8.33 kHz or 25 kHz channel steps.

jeopardy.clue: VHF aircraft communication radios operate in this specific allocated band.

multipleChoice.stem: The VHF aircraft communication band extends over which frequency range?

practice.stem: A technician verifying a COM radio's tuning range consults the VHF aircraft allocation. What band should the receiver cover?

preassessment.stem: VHF aircraft communication radios operate in which frequency range?

questions.learningObjective: Identify the frequency range of VOR and localizer receivers.
questions.fullObjective: Given a question about VOR and localizer receivers, the technician shall identify the operating band as 108.0 MHz to 117.95 MHz.
questions.targetSourceStem: VOR and localizer navigation receivers operate in what frequency range?

flashcard.definition: 108.0 MHz to 117.95 MHz, with odd tenths reserved for localizer channels.

jeopardy.clue: This is the allocated frequency band for VOR and localizer receivers on civil aircraft.

multipleChoice.stem: VOR and localizer navigation receivers are tuned within which frequency range?

practice.stem: Before a VOR/LOC bench check, a technician wants to confirm the receiver's expected tuning range. Which band covers both VOR and localizer channels?

preassessment.stem: VOR and localizer navigation receivers operate in what frequency range?

questions.learningObjective: Identify the fixed frequency used by all marker beacon ground transmitters.
questions.fullObjective: Given a question about marker beacon ground transmitters, the technician shall identify 75 MHz as the single fixed broadcast frequency used by inner, middle, and outer markers.
questions.targetSourceStem: All marker beacon ground transmitters broadcast on what frequency?

flashcard.definition: 75 MHz, common to outer, middle, and inner markers, distinguished only by modulation tone.

jeopardy.clue: Every marker beacon ground transmitter in the national airspace broadcasts on this single fixed frequency.

multipleChoice.stem: Outer, middle, and inner marker beacon ground transmitters all broadcast on which frequency?

practice.stem: A technician is certifying a marker beacon receiver on the ramp. What transmit frequency must the test set output for every marker station type?
choices: 1030 MHz on the transponder interrogation band

preassessment.stem: All marker beacon ground transmitters broadcast on what frequency?

questions.learningObjective: Describe the purpose of keying an HF transmitter after changing frequency.
questions.fullObjective: Given an HF radio after a frequency selection, the technician shall describe that keying the transmitter commands the antenna coupler to tune to the newly selected frequency.
questions.targetSourceStem: After selecting a frequency on an HF radio, the pilot must key the transmitter before speaking. What does this keying accomplish?

flashcard.term: Commands the antenna coupler to tune for low SWR
flashcard.definition: The keying commands the HF antenna coupler to tune itself for a low-SWR match on the new frequency.

jeopardy.clue: This is what the pilot accomplishes by briefly keying an HF transmitter after selecting a new frequency.

multipleChoice.stem: After choosing a new HF frequency, the pilot keys the transmitter briefly before speaking. This action:
choices: Places the weather radar into standby mode
Engages the aircraft autopilot for cruise
Commands the antenna coupler to tune for low SWR
Turns off non-essential cockpit equipment loads

practice.stem: A pilot asks why he is told to key the HF transmitter briefly every time he changes frequency, before speaking. Which explanation is correct?
choices: The key-up signal sheds non-essential cockpit electrical loads.
The key-up signal engages the autopilot into its enroute cruise submode.
The key-up signal drives the HF antenna coupler through an automatic tuning cycle.
The key-up signal places the weather radar into its standby state.

preassessment.stem: Keying an HF transmitter after selecting a new frequency accomplishes what?
choices: It places the weather radar into standby.
It engages the autopilot in cruise submode.
It drives the antenna coupler through a tuning cycle.
It turns off non-essential cockpit loads.

questions.learningObjective: Select test equipment that measures antenna SWR on a COM system.
questions.fullObjective: Given a task to measure the standing wave ratio of a communication antenna system, the technician shall select an inline directional wattmeter as the correct test equipment.
questions.targetSourceStem: What test equipment measures the standing wave ratio of a communication antenna system?

flashcard.definition: An inline directional wattmeter, measuring forward and reflected power between radio and antenna.

jeopardy.clue: This piece of test equipment measures forward and reflected power to yield the standing wave ratio of a COM antenna.

multipleChoice.stem: The standing wave ratio of a communication antenna system is measured using:

practice.stem: A technician plans to verify that a newly installed COM antenna feedline has acceptable SWR. Which piece of test equipment should he connect between the radio and antenna?

preassessment.stem: Which test equipment is used to measure SWR on a COM antenna system?

questions.learningObjective: Diagnose a no-receive VHF COM when transmit works.
questions.fullObjective: Given a VHF COM that transmits normally but receives nothing even at maximum volume, the technician shall first check that the squelch is not set too high.
questions.targetSourceStem: A VHF COM transmits normally but the pilot hears nothing on receive even at maximum volume. What should the technician check first?

flashcard.term: Whether the squelch is adjusted too tight
flashcard.definition: The squelch control; if it is set too high, the receiver remains muted for all valid signals.

jeopardy.clue: When a COM radio transmits normally but produces no receive audio even at full volume, this is the first setting to check.
choices: Squelch may be set too high
PTT switch may be stuck on

multipleChoice.stem: A VHF COM transmits normally but produces no receiver audio at any volume. The technician should check first:
choices: Whether the squelch is adjusted too tight
Whether the antenna has elevated SWR
Whether the final amplifier is weak
Whether the PTT switch is jammed on

practice.stem: A pilot complains that the radio transmits fine but there is complete silence on receive, even with volume at maximum. What should the technician verify before unsoldering anything?
choices: That the squelch threshold is not adjusted too tight
That the antenna feedline does not have elevated SWR
That the transmit final amplifier is not partially weak
That the PTT switch has not become stuck in the on state

preassessment.stem: A VHF COM transmits normally but has no receive audio at any volume. The first thing to check is:

questions.learningObjective: Interpret a VOR NAV flag when the aircraft is within station range.
questions.fullObjective: Given a VOR receiver displaying the NAV flag while within published range of the tuned station, the technician shall interpret this indication as the received signal being below usable threshold.
questions.targetSourceStem: The VOR receiver displays a NAV flag even though the aircraft is within range of the station. What does this indicate?

flashcard.term: Signal has fallen below the usable threshold
flashcard.definition: The received VOR signal level has dropped below the usable threshold required for reliable guidance.

jeopardy.clue: When a VOR shows this flag while the aircraft is within published station range, the signal has dropped below the usable threshold.
choices: A message that no station is tuned
A NAV flag indicating unusable signal
An OBS set to the wrong radial
An autopilot that is not coupled

multipleChoice.stem: A VOR receiver displays the NAV flag while the aircraft is within published range of the tuned station. This indicates:
choices: No station is tuned on the selected frequency
Signal has fallen below the usable threshold
The OBS selector is set to an incorrect radial
The autopilot is not coupled to the NAV source

practice.stem: During a VOR check the pilot notices the NAV flag is in view even though the aircraft is well inside the station's published service volume. What does this indication mean?
choices: The autopilot is simply not coupled to the VOR nav source.
The received VOR signal is below the usable threshold for reliable navigation.
The OBS selector has been set to the wrong desired radial.
No station is currently tuned on the selected VOR frequency.

preassessment.stem: A VOR NAV flag displayed while within station range indicates that:
choices: The autopilot is not coupled to NAV.
The signal level is below the usable threshold.
The OBS is set to the wrong radial.
No station is tuned on the selected frequency.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the cause of a false glideslope indication during an ILS approach.
questions.fullObjective: Given an ILS approach, the technician shall identify side lobes above the correct glidepath as the part of the signal that causes false glideslope indications.
questions.targetSourceStem: During an ILS approach, a false glideslope indication can occur when flying through what part of the signal?

flashcard.term: Upper side lobes of the glideslope radiation pattern
flashcard.definition: Side lobes radiated above the correct 3-degree path can capture the receiver and display a false on-path.

jeopardy.clue: False glideslope captures on ILS approaches come from this part of the transmitted signal.
choices: The main beam at the correct angle
Side lobes above the correct path

multipleChoice.stem: During an ILS approach, a false glideslope indication is most likely to occur when flying through:
choices: The primary main beam at the correct descent angle
The back course signal radiated by the localizer
Upper side lobes of the glideslope radiation pattern
The coverage area of a marker beacon station

practice.stem: A pilot briefing an ILS approach wants to understand where false glideslope indications come from. Which part of the transmitted signal pattern causes them?
choices: The localizer back-course signal off the opposite end
The main beam at the correct three-degree descent angle
Side lobes of the glideslope antenna above the correct path
The conical coverage area of the marker beacon array

preassessment.stem: A false glideslope indication during ILS occurs when flying through:
choices: The main beam at the correct angle
Upper side lobes above the correct path

questions.learningObjective: Explain how DME computes slant range to the ground station.
questions.fullObjective: Given a DME interrogator and transponder pair, the technician shall explain that DME calculates distance by timing the round-trip delay of interrogation and reply pulses.
questions.targetSourceStem: How does distance measuring equipment calculate the distance to the ground station?

flashcard.term: Timing the round-trip delay of its interrogation pulses
flashcard.definition: It times the round-trip delay of its interrogation pulse and the ground transponder's reply pulse.

jeopardy.clue: DME computes slant range using this timing measurement between the airborne interrogator and the ground transponder.

multipleChoice.stem: DME calculates distance to the ground station by:
choices: Detecting the Doppler frequency shift of the carrier
Measuring the amplitude reduction of the returned signal
Comparing phase angles of two received signals
Timing the round-trip delay of its interrogation pulses

practice.stem: A student pilot asks how the DME display reaches a nautical-mile figure. What is the correct technical explanation?
choices: The receiver measures how much the returned signal has dropped in amplitude.
The circuits compare the phase difference between two transmitted signals.
The set detects the Doppler frequency shift produced by aircraft motion.
The interrogator times the round-trip delay of its pulse and the ground reply.

preassessment.stem: DME determines distance to the ground station by:
preassessment.explanation: DME measures slant range by timing the round-trip delay of interrogation pulses sent to the ground transponder.
